chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلُ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُزَوِّجَ ابْنَتَهُ وَ يُرِيدُ أَبُوهُ أَنْ يُزَوِّجَهَا رَجُلًا آخَرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 57 – The man wants to get married off, and his father wants her to get married to another man

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/132)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/132)
text: إِذَا زَوَّجَ الْأَبُ وَ الْجَدُّ كَانَ التَّزْوِيجُ لِلْأَوَّلِ فَإِنْ كَانَ جَمِيعاً فِي حَالٍ وَاحِدَةٍ فَالْجَدُّ أَوْلَى .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, form Hisham Bin Salim, and Muhammad Bin Hakeym,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When the father and the grand-father gives in marriage, the (valid) marriage would be of the first one. So if it was together, in one situation, so the grand-father is higher’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from and Muhammad ibn ‘Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from all from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hisham ibn Salim and Muhammad ibn Flakim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If both father and grandfather give in marriage, the first one’s marriage is effective and if both happens at the same time, the grandfather has the priority.’”
